response.justification: While the candidate demonstrates strong general software development seniority and technical foundation with 15+ years of experience, this role is disqualified by two critical gaps: complete absence of native-level Japanese language proficiency (explicitly required), and a career focused primarily on web/backend engineering rather than embedded firmware and network security. The resume shows minimal experience with embedded systems, no security technology background (MACsec, 802.1X, encryption), and limited networking protocol expertise—all core to this role.
strengths: Senior/Staff-level engineer with 15+ years of diverse software development experience across multiple domains
Strong foundation in software craftsmanship, TDD, and testing practices; demonstrates commitment to code quality
Proven ability to quickly learn new languages and technologies across web, mobile, desktop, and embedded contexts
concerns: No native-level Japanese language proficiency despite being an explicit required qualification
Recent career trajectory (5+ years) heavily weighted toward web/TypeScript backend work, not embedded firmware or security
No demonstrated experience with security technologies (MACsec, 802.1X, encryption, authentication) or network protocols (NETCONF, SNMP) required for the role
